title: Init-scan bilingual templates — driven by knowledge_language config
description: Refactor init-scan baseline templates from EN-only to bilingual (EN + zh-CN), dispatched on `fabric-config.knowledge_language` field added in TASK-002. Currently init-scan emits 5 baseline files (tech-stack, module-structure, readme-first-paragraph, code-style, build-config) with hard-coded EN content. After this task: each of the 5 templates has parallel EN + zh-CN versions; init-scan picks based on config: `'en'` → EN templates; `'zh-CN'` → zh-CN templates; `'match-existing'` → scan existing repo prose, default to EN on empty repo. Add 2 init-scan tests (one per language) + preserve existing default test.

files.change: (1) Locate the 5 EN template strings (tech-stack/module-structure/readme-first-paragraph/code-style/build-config); (2) refactor to a templates registry — `templates[language][slug]` shape with 'en' and 'zh-CN' branches; (3) add `match-existing` resolution helper that scans repo's README/docs and detects majority language (default 'en' on empty); (4) wire knowledge_language config field into the dispatcher — read once at init start, branch all 5 baseline emissions on it; (5) write the 5 zh-CN template variants in M3 style (EN headings + zh-CN body + EN tech terms — same style as TASK-007 dogfood rewrites)

files.path: packages/cli/__tests__/init.test.ts (or wherever init-scan tests live — locate)
files.change: Add 2 new test cases: (a) init with knowledge_language='en' produces EN baselines (matches existing snapshot); (b) init with knowledge_language='zh-CN' produces zh-CN baselines (new snapshot with zh-CN body, EN headings); (c) init with knowledge_language='match-existing' on empty repo defaults to 'en'; preserve any existing default behavior test

implementation: Locate init-scan source: `find packages/cli/src/commands -name 'init.ts'` and grep for current template strings
Read current init.ts to understand template emission structure — likely inline strings or imported constants
Refactor templates into a registry: e.g. `const baselineTemplates: Record<KnowledgeLanguage, Record<string, string>> = { 'en': { 'tech-stack': '...', ... }, 'zh-CN': { 'tech-stack': '...', ... } }`
Write 5 zh-CN template variants — apply M3 style (EN headings, zh-CN body, EN tech terms preserved); use TASK-007 dogfood files as reference for tone

Implement match-existing detection helper: read README.md (if present) + docs/ files; count zh-CN characters vs ASCII; if zh-CN ratio > threshold (e.g. 30%) → 'zh-CN' else 'en'; on empty repo (no README, no docs) → 'en'

Wire config: load fabric-config at init start, read knowledge_language field, resolve 'match-existing' to concrete 'en' or 'zh-CN', dispatch all 5 emissions through `baselineTemplates[resolvedLanguage]`
Update existing tests if they snapshot baseline content (snapshot will change for any non-default language); re-record snapshots for each language
Add 2 new test cases per files[].change item
Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/cli build && pnpm --filter @fabric/cli test` — confirm all green
Run end-to-end on a tmp project with each config value — verify output baseline files match expected language
Coverage check: ≥ 90% on new template registry + dispatcher logic

reference.pattern: Bilingual template dispatch with config-driven resolution + match-existing detection fallback
reference.examples: Match the M3 style established in TASK-007 dogfood rewrites; reuse those entries as zh-CN template content where appropriate

criteria: `grep -E 'zh-CN' packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts` returns ≥ 5 hits (5 zh-CN templates present in source)
Init test with knowledge_language='zh-CN' produces baseline files containing zh-CN characters
Init test with knowledge_language='en' produces baseline files identical to current EN snapshot
Init test with knowledge_language='match-existing' on empty repo defaults to 'en' (verified by output)
All 5 baseline slugs (tech-stack, module-structure, readme-first-paragraph, code-style, build-config) have both EN and zh-CN variants

scope_boundary: OUT OF SCOPE: rewriting the existing 5 dogfood baseline files in this repo (TASK-007 handled that as separate dogfood commit); modifying fabric-config schema (TASK-002); modifying KT-DEC entries (TASK-007); supporting non-en/zh-CN languages (out of scope per Q3 — only 2 explicit + match-existing). Strictly: bilingual templates + dispatcher + 2 new tests.

rationale.chosen_approach: Templates registry indexed by language + match-existing detection with 'en' fallback — config-driven, clean dispatch, no per-file conditional branches
alternatives_considered: Per-file if/else branches (verbose, hard to extend)
External template files in templates/ dir (more files to maintain, complicates packaging)
Embed both languages in same template with placeholder swap (loses M3 style — headings differ)
decision_factors: Registry pattern is greppable and extensible if more languages added later
Match-existing default 'en' is documented and explicit (not magic)
Single-source dispatcher keeps init.ts test surface manageable
rationale.tradeoffs: init.ts file size grows by ~5 zh-CN templates worth of content — accepted; alternative external template files would be cleaner for >2 languages but premature for 2

manual_checks: Run `fab init` on a tmp project with `fabric-config.json` containing `"knowledge_language":"zh-CN"` — verify all 5 baseline files have zh-CN body
Run `fab init` on tmp project with `"knowledge_language":"en"` — verify baselines match current EN snapshot
Run `fab init` on tmp project with `"knowledge_language":"match-existing"` and no README — verify defaults to 'en'
Run `fab init` on tmp project with zh-CN README — verify match-existing detects and uses 'zh-CN'
success_metrics: All 3 language modes produce expected baselines
match-existing default-to-'en' on empty repo is documented in code comment
Coverage ≥ 90% on dispatcher and template registry
unit: templates registry has en + zh-CN keys for all 5 slugs
match-existing detection on empty repo returns 'en'
match-existing detection on zh-CN-heavy README returns 'zh-CN'
dispatcher reads knowledge_language from config and selects correct registry branch
integration: End-to-end fab init produces correct-language baselines per each config value
